heroHeadline: Junta Imposes Martial Law on 60 Towns; China FM Wang Yi Visits Naypyidaw; IDP Camp Child Killed in Hpakant Mortar Strike
heroSubtitle: On April 25, 2026 — Day 1,909 of Myanmar's post-coup civil war — the military junta expanded martial law to 60 cities and townships across Kachin, Kayah, Kayin, Chin, Shan, and Rakhine states, authorizing military courts to try civilians with death sentences — one of the most sweeping legal crackdowns since the February 2021 coup. Chinese Foreign Minister and Politburo member Wang Yi arrived in Naypyidaw for a two-day visit, meeting with junta president Min Aung Hlaing in the highest-level Chinese diplomatic engagement with the SAC in 2026, providing Beijing's critical endorsement of the junta's cosmetic 'civilian rule' transition. On April 23, SAC forces mortared an IDP camp in Hpakant Township, Kachin State, killing 13-year-old Maung Swut Jet Aung while he was studying — the latest in a documented pattern of collective punishment targeting IDPs during the ongoing KIA Hpakant offensive. A junta ceremony on April 24 claiming 517 resistance surrenders was dismissed as 'fabricated propaganda' by Naypyidaw PDF Chief of Staff Bo La Kyar. The SAC's simultaneous prosecution of intensified aerial campaigns (100+ civilian deaths since March 16 per Irrawaddy), martial law expansion, and high-level Chinese diplomatic engagement underscores that Min Aung Hlaing's 'civilian rule' is a legitimization strategy accompanied by accelerating military violence.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa CDM News, Japan Times, Khaosod English, The Nation Thailand, Foreign Policy, Xinhua, BERNAMA, Anadolu Agency, IMNA/Mon News, FDD, China MFA, Shan Herald Agency for News, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, AA, and PDF statements.
